channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia judge blocks ballot counting rule and says county officials must certify election results
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge has blocked a new rule that requires Georgia Election Day ballots to be counted by hand after the close of voting. The ruling came a day after the same judge ruled that county election officials must certify election results by the deadline set in law.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until new funds are approved
item.description: The SBA’s disaster loan program has run out of money, it announced on Tuesday. The agency expects to receive new funding from Congress, and will continue to accept applications in the meantime.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: Harris' Fox interview a bigger risk for Bret Baier: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses the state of the race as Kamala Harris and Donald Trump work to energize key voter groups.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Question 3: 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide
item.description: Dr. Sandra Cosgrove and Kerry Durmick joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to provide a proponent and opponent's view of one of the most contentious issues on the Nevada ballot in November: Question 3.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Intel chair frets about foreign actors spreading hurricane, election misinformation
item.description: Senate Intelligence Committee Chair Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Tuesday indicated that he is concerned foreign actors are spreading misinformation related to the recovery from a pair of recent hurricanes and will be ramping up efforts amid the final election sprint.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Voting rights groups seek investigation into Wisconsin text message
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Voting rights advocates on Tuesday asked state and federal authorities to investigate anonymous text messages apparently targeting young Wisconsin voters, warning them not to vote in a state where they are ineligible.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tillis warns conservatives’ 'unwise' reforms would 'weaken' Senate GOP
item.description: North Carolina Sen. Thom Tillis (R) circulated a “Dear Colleague” letter Tuesday warning that some of the reforms being pushed by Senate conservatives would only weaken the next Senate Republican leader and cause the same chaos that is plaguing the House Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump town hall ends with extended 'musical-fest' while he stands onstage
item.description: At a town hall in Oaks, Pa., on Monday, Trump ended the Q&A portion to ask the audience to remain and listen to music with him.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: In divided St. Cloud, challengers hope to flip House seats
item.description: While many legislative districts in Minnesota are either solidly Republican or DFL, in the St. Cloud, voters are evenly split. Races for the two House seats that represent the region are among the most competitive in the state.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Migrant deaths in New Mexico have increased tenfold
item.description: Experts say smugglers are treating migrants more harshly and bringing them on paths that could be more dangerous in extreme summer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris slams Trump for suggesting military handle 'enemy from within' as they vie for Pennsylvania
item.description: Harris, at a rally in Pennsylvania, called Trump a serious threat to American democracy who is “out for unchecked power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, WILL WEISSERT and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris brings a bit of Trump into her rally to try to show him as ‘unhinged’
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris wants voters to pay more attention when Trump talks about “the enemy within.” On Monday, she played a highlight reel to paint him as “increasingly unstable and unhinged.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: VP hopefuls make Midwest push as Vance pops in on Minnesota, Walz swings through Wisconsin
item.description: A near-meeting on the tarmac at the Minneapolis-St. Paul airport opens the campaign week for Tim Walz and JD Vance, the vice presidential nominees on their party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Jill Biden holds surrogate events for Harris; when will Joe Biden?
item.description: Several Democratic consultants told NewsNation – even with his low approval numbers – Biden can be beneficial to Harris in key areas. .
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: In outreach to Black men, Harris to vow to legalize weed, protect crypto
item.description: Polls show that some Black men may be gravitating toward former President Trump or not vote at all. Vice President Harris and other prominent Democrats are trying to counter that.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: Authorities say a Nevada man with a shotgun and a loaded handgun in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump’s rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump holds a rally in California, a state he's almost certain to lose
item.description: The White House election is expected to come down to battlegrounds such as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tameka Henry discusses oversight, safety, and leadership in Clark County School District race
item.description: Tameka Henry, a candidate for the Clark County Board of Trustees, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform and vision for improving the school district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Who could replace interim NYPD Commissioner Donlon if he resigns?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to resign following a string of high-profile exits from City Hall over the last month, sources told PIX11 News on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Annette Dawson Owens discusses education, healthcare, and affordable housing
item.description: Annette Dawson Owens, a Republican candidate for Nevada’s Assembly District 29, recently discussed her views on education, healthcare, and affordable housing on 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tallahassee sends city crews to aid Tampa Bay recovery efforts
item.description: As communities wait for daylight to come Thursday morning to see the damage that has been done and for emergency personnel to start rescue efforts, counties in north Florida are sending their crews to the Tampa Bay area to aid in recovery.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shondra Summers-Armstrong discusses key issues in Las Vegas city council race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) — Shondra Summers-Armstrong, currently serving as an Assemblywoman for District 6, is running for Las Vegas City Council, Ward 5.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
